Passengers (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $100,014,699Details
International Box Office $202,224,973
Worldwide Box Office $302,239,672
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,465,645
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,425,248
Total $19,890,893
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Two passengers are on a 120-year journey to another planet when their hibernation pods wake them 90 years too early. Jim and Aurora are forced to unravel the mystery behind the malfunction as the ship teeters on the brink of collapse, with the lives of thousands of passengers in jeopardy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,869,736 (14.9% of total gross)
Legs:6.19 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,478 opening theaters/3,478 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $136,084,588

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 21st, 2016 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: December 21st, 2016 (Wide) (Vietnam)
December 21st, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
December 21st, 2016 (Wide) (Trinidad)
December 21st, 2016 (Wide) (Taiwan)
December 21st, 2016 (Wide) (Sweden)
Video Release: March 7th, 2017 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexuality, nudity and action/peril.
(Rating bulletin 2448 (Cert #50101), 10/26/2016)
Running Time: 116 minutes
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion
Geographical setting: Outer Space
Plot point: Disaster, Romance
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, LStar Capital, Village Roadshow Productions, Wanda Media, Original Film, Company Films, Start Motion Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Two passengers are on a 120-year journey to another planet when their hibernation pods wake them 90 years too early. Jim and Aurora are forced to unravel the mystery behind the malfunction as the ship teeters on the brink of collapse, with the lives of thousands of passengers in jeopardy.
